question.text: High interest rates impact commodity prices through all of the following channels EXCEPT:

answer.text: By increasing the demand for physical storage of commodities
By increasing the incentive for extraction today rather than tomorrow
By decreasing firms' desire to carry inventories

explanation.text: **The Logic:** High interest rates reduce commodity prices through 4 channels: (1) incentive for extraction now, (2) decrease inventory-holding desire, (3) shift investments to treasury bills, (4) appreciate domestic currency. High rates DECREASE (not increase) the desire to store commodities. **The Trap:** All other options correctly describe channels. The incorrect option reverses the effect — high rates increase the cost of holding inventory, so demand for storage decreases. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series IV (Interest Rate Derivatives) covers interest rate impact on asset prices extensively. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The interest rate-commodity price relationship is inverse in the medium term — when RBI raises rates, expect downward pressure on commodity prices, especially storable ones like metals.
